title: [v5.6] Bump to Buildah v1.41.2
body:
Bump Buildah to v1.41.2 in preparation for Podman v5.6.0. This cures a last minute issue in the build code.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Can we re-enable the test that #26771 disabled?
Repushed with the test that failed originally, turned back on now.

@mheon @Luap99 @nalind it looks like the sqlite tests are not happy here and in a couple of other PRs.  Is there anything that can be tweaked for them?  I've rerun 3 times without happy green test buttons.

```
           time="2025-08-13T22:57:31Z" level=warning msg="Failed, retrying in 2s ... (1/3). Error: initializing source containers-storage:testimage-working-container: checking which exclusions are in base image \"b82e560ed57b77a897379e160371adcf1b000ca885e69c62cbec674777a83850\": layer not known"
```

That doesn't look like sqlite. Did something happen to our test image?

There's an interaction between https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6332 / https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6337 and podman's use of the imagestore option in its e2e tests that we don't run into in buildah CI because none of its tests have ever used the option.  I've opened https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6340 for it.

Cherry-picked https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6340 onto https://github.com/nalind/buildah/tree/mount-image-fails-with-imagestores-1.41 and vendored that into https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26819.
